prompt:

prompt: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?
questionText: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?

choices.text: A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 11 photons in the beam.
A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 11 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 300 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 600 photons in the beam.

subject: math
prompt: A computer model tracks the population of a certain beetle in an environment where the beetle has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the beetle population at the end of every 5-week period is 140% greater than the estimated total mass at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population at the end of 15 weeks is 591.0 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the beetle population at the end of $t$ weeks?
questionText: A computer model tracks the population of a certain beetle in an environment where the beetle has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the beetle population at the end of every 5-week period is 140% greater than the estimated total mass at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population at the end of 15 weeks is 591.0 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the beetle population at the end of $t$ weeks?

subject: math
prompt: A group of 55 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of movies they watched last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of movies employees at the company watched last year is 8.4, with an associated margin of error of 1.3. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?
questionText: A group of 55 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of movies they watched last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of movies employees at the company watched last year is 8.4, with an associated margin of error of 1.3. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of movies employees at the company watched last year is less than 7.1.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of movies employees at the company watched last year is between 7.1 and 9.7.
It is plausible that every employee at the company watched between 8.4 and 12.3 movies last year.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of movies employees at the company watched last year is greater than 9.7.

category.skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
